title: Convert a prose capability assessment to body-slide bullets
guidance: Reward telegraphic compression that stays parseable. Penalise prose-y output or undefined acronyms.

runner_input.instruction: Rewrite the two paragraphs as body-slide bullets in the telegraphic register.
runner_input.constraints: 4–6 bullets. Abbreviations allowed. Define any acronym once.

source_blocks.text: The engineering team is 18 full-time staff, which is around 27% of the overall headcount, and together with the product and food-safety authoring teams makes up close to 48% of the company. The current ratio is roughly 2 product people to 13 developers, which is likely too development-heavy given the progress in agentic coding, and product is likely to be the bottleneck for fuller adoption of an AI-native software development lifecycle.

Voluntary attrition is low at 6.1% and there were a number of internal promotions over the last year. Glassdoor complaints concentrate on the sales function rather than engineering, and there is consistently positive feedback on the chief technology officer.

rubric_notes: Good output reads like a real consulting body slide: e.g. 'Eng 18 FTE (~27% of co; ~48% incl. product + authors)'.
Define SDLC on first use; don't let density block comprehension.
